title: Robot end effector for fish injection
abstract: In a robot end effector for fish injection, a suction device (832) and fish support (836) are configurable between a first arrangement (not shown) with the fish support (836) swung out of the way to the side, in which the suction device (832) lifts a fish (834) by sucking from above the fish, and a second arrangement (as shown), in which the fish support (836) extends underneath the fish (834) to support the fish. An intra-peritoneal fish injection device (818) is operable to inject the fish with a needle (844) when supported by the fish support (836) in the second arrangement. An intra-muscular injector actuator (804) is provided to actuate a needle (842) into the muscle of a fish when held in the end effector.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A robot end effector for fish injection, the robot end effector comprising: - a suction device operable to lift a fish; - a fish support, wherein the suction device and fish support are configurable between: - a first arrangement in which the suction device lifts the fish; and - a second arrangement in which the fish support extends underneath at least part of the fish to support the fish; and - a fish injection device operable to inject the fish when supported by the fish support in the second arrangement. 2. The end effector of claim 1 , further comprising an actuator to adjust the configuration between the first and second arrangements. 3. The end effector of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the suction device is operable to place the fish on to the fish support. 4. The end effector of any preceding claim, wherein the suction device is operable to lift the fish up by sucking from above the fish. 5. The end effector of any preceding claim, wherein the fish support comprises a perforated fish slice. 6. The end effector of any preceding claim, wherein the suction device and fish support are configurable between the first and second arrangements by a relative rigid motion. 7. The end effector of any preceding claim, wherein the fish support comprises a pair of members shaped to engage with the fish. 8. The end effector of claim 7, wherein the pair of members are moved towards each other into the second arrangement to support the fish. 9. The end effector of any preceding claim, wherein the fish support comprises a convex surface to support the fish. 10. The end effector of claim 9, wherein the suction device and fish support are configurable between the first arrangement and second arrangement by moving the fish support around a pivot in a scooping motion to cradle the fish with a leading portion of the convex surface being further from the pivot than a trailing portion of the convex surface. 11. The end effector of any preceding claim, further comprising an alignment guide operable to guide the fish injection device and fish into alignment for intra-peritoneal injection of the fish. 12. The end effector of claim 11 , wherein the alignment guide is operable to guide the fish injection device and fish into alignment by engagement of the alignment guide with the fish's body. 13. The end effector of claim 12, wherein the alignment guide is biased to engage with the fish. 14. The end effector of claim 13, wherein the alignment guide is biased by gravity to engage with the fish. 15. The end effector of claim 13, wherein the alignment guide is operable to guide the fish injection device and fish into alignment responsive to force feedback from contact of the alignment guide with the fish's body. 16. The end effector of claim 11 , wherein the alignment guide is operable to guide the fish injection device and fish into alignment by engagement of the alignment guide with the fish support. 17. The end effector of claim 16, wherein the alignment guide is operable to guide the fish injection device and fish into alignment by engagement of the alignment guide with an extension of the suction device. 18. The end effector of any of claims 11 to 17, wherein the fish injection device and alignment guide are fixed together in an assembly. 19. The end effector of any of claims 11 to 18, wherein the alignment guide comprises the fish support. 20. A method of operating a robot end effector for fish injection comprising: - configuring a suction device and a fish support in a first arrangement; - lifting a fish with the suction device; - configuring the suction device and fish support in a second arrangement in which the fish support extends underneath at least part of the fish to support the fish; and - injecting the fish when supported by the fish support in the second arrangement. 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising placing the fish on to the fish support using the suction device. 22. The method of claim 20 or 21 , further comprising guiding a fish injection device and fish into alignment for intra-peritoneal injection of the fish. 23. The method of claim 22, further comprising guiding the fish injection device and fish into alignment for intra-peritoneal injection of the fish by engagement of the alignment guide with the fish's body. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising biasing the alignment guide to engage with the fish. 25. The method of claim 24, further comprising biasing the alignment guide to engage with the fish by gravity. 26. The method of claim 23, further comprising guiding the fish injection device and fish into alignment responsive to force feedback from contact of the alignment guide with the fish's body. 27. The method of claim 22, further comprising guiding the fish injection device and fish into alignment by engagement of the alignment guide with the fish support. 28. The method of claim 27, further comprising guiding the fish injection device and fish into alignment by engagement of an alignment guide with an extension of the suction device.
